title: Viking Therapeutics Q1 2026 Earnings Call Highlights
text: Viking Therapeutics reported Q1 2026 results with substantial losses driven by elevated R&D expenses. The company posted a net loss of $158.3 million compared to $45.6 million in the prior-year period, with R&D expenses jumping to $115.2 million versus $41.4 million year-over-year. Despite the losses, the company maintains a strong cash position of $603 million, which management projects will fund operations into 2028 through key catalysts. The Phase 3 VANQUISH program achieved full enrollment with approximately 4,500 patients in VANQUISH-1 and 1,000 in VANQUISH-2. The company transitioned study participants from traditional vial-and-syringe administration to an auto-injector format. For oral formulation development, Viking plans to initiate Phase 3 testing in Q4 2026, targeting a maximum of two tablets for higher doses to address patient preferences from earlier trials. The maintenance dosing study was expanded from four to eight subcutaneous cohorts, evaluating various dosing regimens. The company expects subcutaneous results in Q3 2026, with oral maintenance data anticipated in the first half of 2027. Viking filed an investigational new drug application for VK3019, an amylin receptor agonist, with Phase 1 testing expected pending FDA clearance.
